title: 修复错误将chunk_size更新为chunks_number的问题
body:
KnowledgeBaseManager类中，更新File表的chunk_number字段使用的方法update_chunks_number存在sql语句错误。
实际为"UPDATE File SET chunk_size = %s WHERE file_id = %s"。这会错误地修改chunk_size，导致chunk_size和chunks_number值相同。

同时，在文件插入向量数据库结束后，原代码会有针对chunks_number等信息的统一修改。

所以可以将insert_files_server.py中，process_data中对chunks_number做的更新语句：mysql_client.update_chunks_number(local_file.file_id, chunks_number)进行删除。
修改后，文件上传成功后chunks_number和chunk_size的值完全正确。

step_plan.summary: 修复 KnowledgeBaseManager.update_chunks_number 的 SQL 字段错误，避免把 chunks_number 写到 chunk_size 上；同时移除入库流程中对 chunks_number 的重复更新，统一由后续的集中 UPDATE 写入，保证上传后 chunk_size 与 chunks_number 元数据正确。

steps.operation: Fix UPDATE statement to write to the correct column (chunks_number) instead of chunk_size
steps.reason: The current SQL mistakenly updates `File.chunk_size`, causing `chunk_size` and `chunks_number` to become identical and corrupting metadata. Correcting the column to `chunks_number` fixes the root data bug.
steps.side_effects: Any code path calling `update_chunks_number` will now correctly modify `chunks_number` (behavior change vs. previous buggy behavior). If any downstream logic accidentally relied on the wrong overwrite of `chunk_size`, it will no longer occur; verify UI/metadata displays and any reporting that uses `chunk_size`.

steps.operation: Remove redundant chunks_number update call after vector DB insertion; rely on the existing unified MySQL update later in the pipeline
steps.reason: `process_data()` currently calls `mysql_client.update_chunks_number(local_file.file_id, chunks_number)` even though the surrounding flow later performs a unified UPDATE that includes `chunks_number`. Removing this avoids duplicate writes and potential race/overwrite issues, and ensures `chunk_size` is not accidentally impacted by the buggy method in older versions.
steps.side_effects: `chunks_number` will be persisted only at the unified update point; if any intermediate consumer expected `chunks_number` to be available in MySQL immediately after Milvus insertion (before the unified update), that timing changes. Fewer MySQL writes per file may slightly improve throughput; ensure any status/progress logic doesn’t depend on early `chunks_number` persistence.
